metadata.methodology: BNG permission counts reflect planning applications with mandatory or voluntary biodiversity net gain conditions in England. Biodiversity units traded includes both statutory and voluntary market transactions recorded through the Natural England register and major habitat banks. Habitat hectares created covers new or restored habitat secured through BNG obligations with 30-year management plans. Local authority readiness is based on DEFRA survey of planning authorities. Credit prices are median transaction prices from Environment Bank and comparable registries.
knownIssues: Pre-2024 data includes voluntary BNG adoptions only — mandatory requirement commenced February 2024 for major developments, February 2025 for small sites
Biodiversity unit quality and additionality remain contested — traded units may not compensate for locally irreplaceable habitats
30-year management obligation monitoring is not yet systematically tracked
Credit price data is indicative — market is illiquid and prices vary significantly by habitat type and geography
